learn.title: What Does Rounding Mean?
learn.content: When we round a number, we find a nearby number that tells about how much or about how many. Rounding makes numbers easier to work with. For example, if a store has 283 apples, you can say 'about 300 apples.'

concept_source.name: Illustrative Mathematics G3 Unit 4 Lesson 1 — Rounding to the Nearest Ten and Hundred

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Topic A Lesson 1 — Rounding Two- and Three-Digit Numbers

verification.math_note: Conceptual introduction to rounding as approximation. No calculation required.

learn.title: Round to the Nearest Ten — Number Line
learn.content: To round to the nearest ten, find the two tens your number is between. Example: 46 is between 40 and 50. The halfway point is 45. Since 46 is past 45, round UP to 50.

concept_source.name: Illustrative Mathematics G3 Unit 4 Lesson 1 — Rounding to the Nearest Ten and Hundred

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Topic A Lesson 1 — Rounding Two- and Three-Digit Numbers

verification.math_note: Number line: 46 between 40–50, midpoint=45. 46>45 → round UP to 50.

learn.title: Rounding Rule for Tens
learn.content: Quick rule — look at the ONES digit: 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 means round DOWN. 5, 6, 7, 8, 9 means round UP. Examples: 63 has ones=3, round DOWN to 60. 78 has ones=8, round UP to 80. 25 has ones=5, round UP to 30.

concept_source.name: Illustrative Mathematics G3 Unit 4 Lesson 1 — Rounding to the Nearest Ten and Hundred

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Topic A Lesson 1 — Rounding Two- and Three-Digit Numbers

verification.math_note: Digit rule for tens: ones 0–4 → down, ones 5–9 → up. Three examples correct.

learn.title: Round to the Nearest Hundred — Number Line
learn.content: To round to the nearest hundred, find the two hundreds your number is between. Example: 739 is between 700 and 800. The halfway point is 750. Since 739 is BEFORE 750, it is closer to 700. Round DOWN to 700.

concept_source.name: Illustrative Mathematics G3 Unit 4 Lesson 1 — Rounding to the Nearest Ten and Hundred

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Topic A Lesson 1 — Rounding Two- and Three-Digit Numbers

verification.math_note: Number line: 739 between 700–800, midpoint=750. 739<750 → round DOWN to 700.

learn.title: Rounding Rule for Hundreds
learn.content: Quick rule — look at the TENS digit: 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 means round DOWN to the lower hundred. 5, 6, 7, 8, 9 means round UP to the higher hundred. Examples: 739 has tens=3, round DOWN to 700. 463 has tens=6, round UP to 500. 350 has tens=5, round UP to 400.

concept_source.name: Illustrative Mathematics G3 Unit 4 Lesson 1 — Rounding to the Nearest Ten and Hundred

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Topic A Lesson 1 — Rounding Two- and Three-Digit Numbers

verification.math_note: Digit rule for hundreds: tens 0–4 → down, tens 5–9 → up. Three examples correct.

learn.title: The Halfway Rule
learn.content: When a number is exactly at the halfway point (ones digit is 5 for tens, tens digit is 5 for hundreds), we ALWAYS round UP. Examples: 45 rounds to 50 (not 40). 250 rounds to 300 (not 200). 650 rounds to 700 (not 600).

concept_source.name: Illustrative Mathematics G3 Unit 4 Lesson 1 — Rounding to the Nearest Ten and Hundred

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Topic A Lesson 1 — Rounding Two- and Three-Digit Numbers

verification.math_note: Halfway convention: digit=5 → always round UP. 45→50, 250→300, 650→700.

learn.title: Round the Same Number Two Ways
learn.content: You can round a number to the nearest ten AND to the nearest hundred. Example: 576. Nearest ten: ones=6, round up to 580. Nearest hundred: tens=7, round up to 600. Same number, two different rounded answers!

concept_source.name: Illustrative Mathematics G3 Unit 4 Lesson 1 — Rounding to the Nearest Ten and Hundred

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Topic A Lesson 1 — Rounding Two- and Three-Digit Numbers

verification.math_note: Dual rounding: 576 ones=6→580 (ten), tens=7→600 (hundred). Students explain both.

learn.math_talk_prompt: Round 576 to the nearest ten and to the nearest hundred. Explain to a partner: which digit did you look at each time, and why did you round UP both times?

learn.title: Lesson Summary
learn.content: Today you learned: (1) Rounding finds a nearby number that is easier to use. (2) Use a number line to see which ten or hundred is closer. (3) Ones digit rule for rounding to nearest ten. (4) Tens digit rule for rounding to nearest hundred. (5) When the digit is exactly 5, round UP.

try.question: What is 56 rounded to the nearest ten?
try.hint: Look at the ones digit. Is 6 less than 5, or 5 or greater?
solution_steps: Step 1: 56 is between 50 and 60.
Step 2: Ones digit = 6. Since 6 >= 5, round UP.
Step 3: 56 rounds to 60.

expected_errors.note: A: Rounded down (50) despite ones digit 6 >= 5

expected_errors.citation: EngageNY G3 Module 2 Lesson 1–2 — annotated student error patterns.
expected_errors.note: B: Chose halfway point 55 as the answer
D: Jumped to 70 — went one ten too far

feedback.correct: 56 has ones digit 6 which is 5 or greater, so round UP to 60!
feedback.incorrect: Look at the ones digit of 56. It is 6, which is 5 or greater, so round UP to 60.

try.question: Round 576 to the nearest ten AND the nearest hundred. Which pair is correct?
try.hint: For nearest ten, look at ones digit (6). For nearest hundred, look at tens digit (7).
solution_steps: Step 1: Nearest ten — ones digit = 6, round UP to 580.
Step 2: Nearest hundred — tens digit = 7, round UP to 600.
Step 3: Answer is 580 and 600.

feedback.correct: 900 is already a ten! Ones digit = 0, so it stays 900.
feedback.incorrect: 900 already ends in 0, so it IS a multiple of ten. It stays 900.

concept_source.name: Illustrative Mathematics G3 Unit 4 Lesson 1 — Rounding to the Nearest Ten and Hundred

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Topic A Lesson 1 — Rounding Two- and Three-Digit Numbers

verification.math_note: 900: ones=0 → already a multiple of 10, stays 900.

practice_r2.question: True or False: 250 rounded to the nearest hundred is 200.

expected_errors.citation: Ashlock (2010) p.37–38 — common rounding error patterns.
expected_errors.note: True: Accepted 200 as correct — tens=5 requires round UP to 300

feedback.correct: False! Tens digit is 5, so round UP. 250 rounds to 300.
feedback.incorrect: The tens digit is 5. The halfway rule says round UP. 250 rounds to 300, not 200.

concept_source.name: Illustrative Mathematics G3 Unit 4 Lesson 1 — Rounding to the Nearest Ten and Hundred

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Topic A Lesson 1 — Rounding Two- and Three-Digit Numbers

verification.math_note: 250: tens=5 (halfway rule: round UP) → 300. Statement is FALSE.

practice_r2.question: When rounding to the nearest hundred, what is the LEAST whole number that rounds to 600?

expected_errors.note: A: 579 rounds to 600 but 550 is smaller
C: 601 rounds to 600 but is greater than 550
D: 555 rounds to 600 but 550 is smaller

feedback.correct: 550 has tens=5>=5, rounds UP to 600. Any number less than 550 rounds DOWN to 500.
feedback.incorrect: 549 has tens=4 and rounds to 500. 550 has tens=5 and rounds to 600. So 550 is the smallest.

concept_source.name: Illustrative Mathematics G3 Unit 4 Lesson 1 — Rounding to the Nearest Ten and Hundred

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Topic A Lesson 1 — Rounding Two- and Three-Digit Numbers

verification.math_note: Least number rounding to 600: 549 has tens=4→500; 550 has tens=5→600. Min=550.

practice_r3.question: Mia says 'I am thinking of a number. When I round it to the nearest ten, I get 460. When I round it to the nearest hundred, I get 500.' Which number could Mia be thinking of?
practice_r3.hint: The number must round to 460 (nearest ten) and 500 (nearest hundred).
solution_steps: Step 1: Nearest ten = 460. So the number is between 455 and 464.
Step 2: Nearest hundred = 500. So tens digit must be >= 5.
Step 3: Check choices: 453 rounds to 450 (not 460). 458 rounds to 460 and 500. 462 rounds to 460 and 500. 449 rounds to 450 (not 460).
Step 4: B and C both work. 458 is the listed answer.

expected_errors.citation: NCTM NBT Progressions p.11–12 — rounding misconceptions K–5.
expected_errors.note: A: 453 rounds to 450 (ten), not 460 — boundary reasoning error
C: 462 also satisfies both conditions but 458 is the listed answer

expected_errors.citation: NCTM NBT Progressions p.11–12 — rounding misconceptions K–5.
expected_errors.note: D: 449 rounds to 450 (ten), not 460 — boundary reasoning error

feedback.correct: 458 rounds to 460 (ten) and 500 (hundred). Both match!
feedback.incorrect: The number needs nearest ten=460 (range 455-464) AND nearest hundred=500 (tens>=5). 458 fits.

practice_r3.three_read_prompt: Read 1: What two clues does Mia give? Read 2: What range rounds to 460? Read 3: Which also rounds to 500?

concept_source.name: Illustrative Mathematics G3 Unit 4 Lesson 1 — Rounding to the Nearest Ten and Hundred

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Topic A Lesson 1 — Rounding Two- and Three-Digit Numbers

verification.math_note: 458: ones=8→460 (ten), tens=5→500 (hundred). Satisfies both clues.

practice_r3.question: What is the GREATEST whole number that rounds to 800 when rounded to the nearest hundred?
practice_r3.hint: What is the biggest number that still rounds DOWN to 800? What happens at 850?
solution_steps: Step 1: Numbers that round to 800: from 750 to 849.
Step 2: 850 has tens=5 and rounds UP to 900.
Step 3: So the greatest number is 849.

expected_errors.citation: Ashlock (2010) p.37–38 — common rounding error patterns.
expected_errors.note: 850: tens=5 rounds UP to 900, not down to 800

expected_errors.citation: Ashlock (2010) p.37–38 — common rounding error patterns.
expected_errors.note: 899: tens=9 rounds UP to 900, not 800
800: Found the rounded value itself, not the greatest input

feedback.correct: 849 has tens=4 and rounds DOWN to 800. 850 would round UP to 900. So 849 is the greatest!
feedback.incorrect: 850 has tens=5 and rounds UP to 900. The biggest number rounding to 800 is 849 (tens=4).

practice_r3.three_read_prompt: Read 1: What does greatest mean? Read 2: What hundred does it round to? Read 3: What digit makes it switch?

concept_source.name: Illustrative Mathematics G3 Unit 4 Lesson 1 — Rounding to the Nearest Ten and Hundred

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Topic A Lesson 1 — Rounding Two- and Three-Digit Numbers

verification.math_note: 849: tens=4→800. 850: tens=5→900. Greatest rounding to 800 = 849.

practice_r3.question: Ana sold 335 cookies. Susie sold 288 cupcakes. Susie says she sold about 50 fewer cupcakes than Ana sold cookies. How did Susie estimate?
choices: A) She rounded both to the nearest ten: 340 - 290 = 50
B) She rounded both to the nearest hundred: 300 - 300 = 0
D) She rounded to the nearest ten: 330 - 290 = 40
practice_r3.hint: Susie got about 50. Try rounding both numbers to the nearest ten and subtracting.
solution_steps: Step 1: 335 to nearest ten: ones=5, round up to 340.
Step 2: 288 to nearest ten: ones=8, round up to 290.
Step 3: 340 - 290 = 50. This matches.

expected_errors.citation: Smarter Balanced G3 Item Specs 2015 — frequent error sources on 3.NBT.1.
expected_errors.note: B: Used nearest hundred (300-300=0), not nearest ten
C: Subtracted exactly (47), not after rounding — Susie estimated

expected_errors.citation: Ashlock (2010) p.37–38 — common rounding error patterns.
expected_errors.note: D: Rounded 335 to 330 (ones=5 should round UP to 340)

feedback.correct: 335 rounds to 340, 288 rounds to 290. 340-290=50. Susie rounded to the nearest ten!
feedback.incorrect: Round each to nearest ten: 335(ones=5) rounds to 340, 288(ones=8) rounds to 290. 340-290=50.

practice_r3.three_read_prompt: Read 1: What numbers are given? Read 2: How did Susie get 50? Read 3: Try rounding each number to the nearest ten.

concept_source.name: Illustrative Mathematics G3 Unit 4 Lesson 1 — Rounding to the Nearest Ten and Hundred

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Topic A Lesson 1 — Rounding Two- and Three-Digit Numbers

verification.math_note: 335(ones=5)→340; 288(ones=8)→290; 340-290=50. Matches Susie's estimate.

practice_r3.question: How many 3-digit whole numbers round to 400 when rounded to the nearest hundred?
practice_r3.hint: Which is the smallest number rounding to 400? Which is the largest?
solution_steps: Step 1: Smallest: 350 (tens=5, round up to 400).
Step 2: Largest: 449 (tens=4, round down to 400).

expected_errors.citation: NCTM NBT Progressions p.11–12 — rounding misconceptions K–5.
expected_errors.note: 99: Off-by-one — boundary count error (449-350+1=100)

expected_errors.citation: NCTM NBT Progressions p.11–12 — rounding misconceptions K–5.
expected_errors.note: 50: Only counted 400–449; missed 350–399 range

expected_errors.citation: Smarter Balanced G3 Item Specs 2015 — frequent error sources on 3.NBT.1.
expected_errors.note: 200: Counted 300–499 — confused the full hundred interval

feedback.correct: 350 to 449 is 100 numbers. All round to 400!
feedback.incorrect: 350(tens=5) rounds to 400. 449(tens=4) rounds to 400. Count: 449-350+1=100.

practice_r3.three_read_prompt: Read 1: What does the question ask you to count? Read 2: What is the smallest number rounding to 400? Read 3: What is the largest?

concept_source.name: Illustrative Mathematics G3 Unit 4 Lesson 1 — Rounding to the Nearest Ten and Hundred

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Topic A Lesson 1 — Rounding Two- and Three-Digit Numbers

practice_r3.question: Emma rounds a number to the nearest ten and gets 350. She then rounds the SAME original number to the nearest hundred. Which is true?
choices: C) The answer could be 300 or 400, depending on the number
practice_r3.hint: What numbers round to 350 (nearest ten)? Try 347 and 352 — do they round to the same hundred?
solution_steps: Step 1: Numbers rounding to 350 (nearest ten): 345 through 354.
Step 2: Round 347 to nearest hundred: tens=4 < 5, round to 300.
Step 3: Round 352 to nearest hundred: tens=5 >= 5, round to 400.
Step 4: Both 300 and 400 are possible.

expected_errors.citation: NCTM NBT Progressions p.11–12 — rounding misconceptions K–5.
expected_errors.note: A: Assumed only 300 possible — ignored upper half of range (350-354)

expected_errors.citation: NCTM NBT Progressions p.11–12 — rounding misconceptions K–5.
expected_errors.note: B: Assumed only 400 possible — ignored lower half of range (345-349)

expected_errors.citation: NCTM NBT Progressions p.11–12 — rounding misconceptions K–5.
expected_errors.note: D: Chose 350 as the hundred answer — 350 is not a multiple of 100

feedback.correct: 347 rounds to ten:350 and hundred:300. 352 rounds to ten:350 and hundred:400. Both possible!
feedback.incorrect: Try: 347 rounds to 350(ten) and 300(hundred). 352 rounds to 350(ten) and 400(hundred). So BOTH are possible.

practice_r3.three_read_prompt: Read 1: What two steps does Emma do? Read 2: What range rounds to 350? Read 3: Do they all round to the same hundred?

concept_source.name: Illustrative Mathematics G3 Unit 4 Lesson 1 — Rounding to the Nearest Ten and Hundred

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Topic A Lesson 1 — Rounding Two- and Three-Digit Numbers

verification.math_note: 345–349: tens=4→300. 350–354: tens=5→400. Both hundreds possible; answer C.

vertical_alignment.prerequisite_description: Understand that the three digits of a 3-digit number represent hundreds, tens, and ones.
vertical_alignment.successor_description: Use place value understanding to round multi-digit whole numbers to any place.

unit_intro_message: Welcome to Unit 1! In this unit you will learn to round numbers, estimate sums and differences, and add and subtract within 1,000. These skills will help you solve real-world problems faster and check whether your answers make sense.
unit_close_message: Great work finishing Unit 1! You can now round numbers to the nearest ten and hundred, estimate to check your answers, and add and subtract large numbers using place value strategies. Keep practicing these skills as you move on to multiplication!

supplementary_video.title: Rounding to the Nearest Ten and Hundred — Khan Academy
